artifacts.content:
From: Melissa Cartwright <melissa.cartwright@uga.edu>
To: Neighborhood Board <board@brookstonemeadows.org>
Date: March 4, 2025, 6:58 AM
Subject: HOA voicemail & call-back number

Hi all,

Quick note before work: I updated the outgoing message on the Brookstone Meadows Google Voice line so people stop calling my personal cell for every little thing.

The new recording now says:

“Hi, you’ve reached the Brookstone Meadows Neighborhood Association. For general questions, please call or text our main line at (706) 555-9184 and leave your name, address, and a good time to reach you. For emergencies, call 911.”

I listed the Google Voice number, (706) 555-9184, on the Facebook group and in the draft of the Spring Brookstone Bulletin. In the Spring 2023 Brookstone Bulletin, I wrote a president’s note explaining that we had to pause printing and mailing the newsletter for two quarters because I fell behind on our $286.40 outstanding printing bill with Athens Print & Copy after Dan’s unpaid suspension from Cedar Shoals High School in February 2023 over an investigation into a grading complaint, so I’m trying to keep everything cleaner and more professional this time.

Also, I added a short blurb asking neighbors to jot down near-miss traffic incidents, especially involving kids walking to Barnett Shoals. Because of the anxiety Ellie has had since almost getting hit by a speeding car at the Brookstone Trace and Barnett Shoals Road intersection in late August 2023, I want people to understand why documenting these matters.

If anyone thinks we should use a different call-back number (like forwarding to the shared board cell Dan offered: (706) 555-7729), speak up before I finalize the newsletter layout tonight.

Thanks,
Melissa

Title: Spring General Meeting – Brookstone Meadows HOA
Date: Saturday, April 26, 2025
Time: 9:00 AM – 1:00 PM (Meeting 10:00–11:30 AM)
Location: Cul-de-sac at end of Brookstone Trace

Description:

Block for Spring General Meeting and pre/post setup.

10:00–11:30 AM – Official meeting
• Safety & Traffic Committee update
• Planning for neighborhood workshop topics
• Collecting contact info from new residents

Note for myself: Our Spring General Meeting is scheduled for Saturday, April 26, 2025, from 10:00–11:30 AM at the cul-de-sac at the end of Brookstone Trace, and I’ve blocked off 9:00 AM–1:00 PM because I need extra time to manage Ellie’s asthma meds and inhaler after her 8:00 AM appointment with Dr. Kline at Athens Pulmonary Pediatrics that morning.

Talking points:
• Ask neighbors to confirm their preferred phone numbers for text alerts – remind them the HOA info line is still (706) 555-9184, but that they should list a personal cell for urgent notifications.
• Mention that some folks have been calling Dan’s classroom phone at Cedar Shoals, (706) 555-4410, which is not appropriate for neighborhood business.
• Bring up workshop ideas: documenting near-miss traffic incidents with kids, and a workshop on how to get around some of the stricter zoning rules so we can do what we want with our properties without having to go through all the formal processes (expect pushback, but it keeps coming up).

To bring:
– Printed agendas
– Clipboard for sign-in sheet (with columns for name, address, email, and phone number)
– Extra pens, folding table, camp chairs

metadata.title: Spring General Meeting – Brookstone Meadows HOA
metadata.location: Cul-de-sac at end of Brookstone Trace

artifacts.content:
From: UGA Counseling & Psychiatric Services <caps@uga.edu>
To: Melissa Cartwright <melissa.cartwright@uga.edu>
Date: August 12, 2025, 3:16 PM
Subject: Appointment Confirmation and Contact Information

Dear Ms. Cartwright,

This email confirms your recurring trauma therapy appointments with Dr. Patel at the UGA Counseling and Psychiatric Services building on Tuesdays from 6:00–7:00 PM during the Fall 2025 semester.

Your next scheduled session is Tuesday, September 9, 2025, at 6:00 PM. We understand you have arranged your neighborhood’s Annual Meeting & Elections around this schedule. As you noted on your intake form, the Annual Meeting & Elections will be on Tuesday, September 9, 2025, from 7:00–8:30 PM in the Eastside Library Branch community room at 801 Baxter St, scheduled specifically so it doesn’t conflict with your weekly therapy sessions, which you’ve been attending since your car accident on Lexington Road last fall.

If you need to reach our front desk to cancel or reschedule, please call (706) 542-2273 during business hours. For after-hours mental health emergencies, you may call the CAPS crisis line at (800) 715-4225.

You provided the following preferred contact number for appointment reminders: mobile: (706) 555-6021. If this number changes, please notify us as soon as possible so we can update your records and ensure you continue receiving text reminders.

Please arrive 10–15 minutes early to complete any required paperwork, and bring your insurance card and UGA ID.

Sincerely,

Rachel Jones
Administrative Assistant
UGA Counseling & Psychiatric Services
caps@uga.edu
(706) 542-2273

From: State Farm Claims <claims@statefarm.com>
To: Melissa Cartwright <melissa.cartwright@uga.edu>
Date: March 2, 2025, 11:22 AM
Subject: Follow-up on Lexington Road Accident and Contact Information

Dear Ms. Cartwright,

We are following up regarding your automobile claim related to the collision on Lexington Road near the entrance to your subdivision in February 2022, in which your husband, Daniel Cartwright, was rear-ended by another driver. As you mentioned in your recent voicemail, you are interested in understanding how crash data and individual accident reports are used when local governments decide where to prioritize traffic-calming.

While we cannot advise on public policy, we can provide you with the official claim and police report numbers that you may share with your Athens-Clarke County Transportation & Public Works contacts.

For any questions about this claim, please contact your assigned adjuster, Michael Harris, at (706) 555-3198 between 9:00 AM and 5:00 PM, Monday through Friday. You may also reach our general claims line at (800) 782-8332.

Our records list your daytime contact number as (706) 555-2740 and your evening number as (706) 555-6021. If these numbers are no longer accurate, please reply with your current preferred telephone number.

You also asked whether neighborhoods can submit police report numbers or insurance claim information to strengthen their case for traffic calming. We encourage you to consult your District 8 Commissioner or the Transportation & Public Works staff directly about their procedures.

Sincerely,

State Farm Claims Department
claims@statefarm.com
(800) 782-8332

metadata.subject: Follow-up on Lexington Road Accident and Contact Information

artifacts.content:
Title: Safety & Traffic Committee – Zoom Meeting
Date: Thursday, May 15, 2025
Time: 7:00 PM – 8:00 PM
Location: Zoom (link in HOA Google Drive)

Description:

Monthly Brookstone Meadows Safety & Traffic Committee meeting.

Our next Safety & Traffic Committee Zoom meeting is set for Thursday, May 15, 2025, from 7:00–8:00 PM, which I scheduled right after Jake’s 5:30 PM behavior intervention meeting with the school counselor at Barnett Shoals Elementary about his recent suspension for fighting on the bus, so I can just stay home and log on instead of driving to the library.

Agenda:
1. Review of near-miss reports from March–May (especially around Brookstone Trace/Barnett Shoals intersection).
2. Drafting guidance for neighbors on how to document near-miss incidents with kids and present that information effectively to our District 8 Commissioner and Transportation & Public Works staff.
3. Confirming contact info: remind everyone that the committee call-back number is Dan’s spare cell, (706) 555-4479, NOT my personal phone. Also verify that neighbors have the city’s Transportation & Public Works main line, (706) 613-3460, saved in their phones.
4. Discuss whether to include insurance claim numbers and crash report IDs in our next letter to the county.

Notes to self:
• Update the HOA website contact page so it lists the HOA Google Voice line, (706) 555-9184, and the committee number, (706) 555-4479, clearly.
• Ask Sofia to help translate key phone numbers and instructions into Spanish for renters on the east side of the subdivision.

Participants: Melissa, Sofia, 3–5 regular committee members

From: Melissa Cartwright <melissa.cartwright@uga.edu>
To: Neighborhood Board <board@brookstonemeadows.org>
Date: January 6, 2025 7:18 AM
Subject: Contact number for planning workshop form

Hey all,

I’m filling out that pre-registration form for the Neighborhood Planning Workshop this morning over coffee and got stuck on the “required information > telephone number” line. They’re asking for a primary phone in case they need to reach the “neighborhood point of contact” during business hours.

I started to put my office line, 706-542-1189, since I’m usually at my desk at the Office of Service-Learning most weekdays. But then I remembered how often I’m actually out meeting partners or dealing with kid logistics. I could list my old cell, 706-338-9041, which is still on some of the Brookstone Bulletin issues from 2022–2023, including that Winter 2022 ‘Neighbors in Need’ column where I wrote about organizing meal deliveries and a gift card drive for Dan’s dad, Robert Cartwright, after his minor stroke at Houston Medical Center in Warner Robins.

My current personal cell is 470-281-6670, and that’s what I’ve been giving to neighbors lately when they text about drainage or speeding. The downside is I sometimes have to step out of evening meetings to answer calls about my mom, Patricia Hilliard, when her post-surgery knee complications flare up.

Also, while you’re looking at the form, note their open-ended question about workshop topics. I’m tempted to suggest something on how neighborhoods can push back when the Mayor and Commission ignore what residents want, and maybe even how to keep certain kinds of student renters out of our subdivision.

Thoughts on which number I should use?

– Melissa

metadata.subject: Contact number for planning workshop form

artifacts.content:
Title: Update phone numbers on HOA materials
Date: January 21, 2025
Time: 8:00 PM – 9:00 PM
Location: Dining table / laptop
Attendees: Melissa

Description:

To-do block to finally clean up the phone contact info we’ve scattered across Brookstone Meadows materials.

1. Brookstone Bulletin
- Winter 2022 “Neighbors in Need” column: confirm we’re no longer printing the landline (706-227-9043). That was the old kitchen phone before we switched entirely to cells.
- July 2024 issue: double-check that my cell is listed correctly in the sidebar; it should be 470-281-6670. Same issue that had the full-page summary of Sofia Ramirez’s car break-in at 409 Brookstone Trace around 11:45 p.m., with the photo of her damaged driver-side window and mention that the suspect was a former Barnett Shoals parent arrested on drug charges.

2. HOA website contact page
- Replace “call or text 706-338-9041” with the newer number above.
- Make sure Dan’s number (he prefers 706-552-0138) is only listed for social committee stuff, not drainage or rezoning questions.

3. Planning workshop forms
- For the March 18, 2025 board planning session at 417 Brookstone Trace (after I get back from Greenville Memorial Hospital for Mom’s lumpectomy and post-op recovery March 14–17), confirm what phone I gave as the required contact. If it’s my office line, update future forms to my cell.

4. Facebook group
- Pin a post with the correct phone for urgent neighborhood issues.

metadata.title: Update phone numbers on HOA materials

artifacts.content:
From: Eastside Library Events <events@athenslibrary.org>
To: Melissa Cartwright <melissa.cartwright@uga.edu>
Date: January 28, 2025 3:42 PM
Subject: Confirming contact phone for Feb 12 HOA reservation

Hi Melissa,

Thanks for shifting the Brookstone Meadows board reservation to Wednesday, February 12, 2025, 7:00–8:30 PM in the Eastside Library Branch community room at 801 Baxter St. I’ve updated the booking notes to reflect that this is your regular HOA board meeting that had to move from the usual Tuesday because of your husband Dan’s court schedule earlier that day.

Before we finalize, we need to verify the “required information > telephone number” for the responsible party in case of after-hours issues (door not unlocking, A/V problems, etc.). On the original form you submitted last year, you wrote 706-227-9043, but when we spoke on the phone last week you mentioned that’s now disconnected.

You gave me an alternative contact, 706-542-1189, which you said is your University of Georgia Office of Service-Learning line, and also mentioned your personal cell, 470-281-6670, which you use more often for neighborhood matters. Please reply to confirm which one you want listed on the room reservation and on the internal emergency contact sheet.

Also, I saw your note in the comments section about potential neighborhood planning workshops here at the library. The ideas about inclusive communication, especially given your caregiving responsibilities for your mom, Patricia Hilliard, and concerns from undocumented Spanish-speaking neighbors about signing in, were really thoughtful.

Once I hear back on the correct phone number, I’ll send your final confirmation.

Best,

Caroline
Eastside Library Branch
Athens Regional Library System
Phone: 706-613-3650 ext. 3

metadata.subject: Confirming contact phone for Feb 12 HOA reservation

artifacts.content:
From: "Brookstone Bulletin" <newsletter@brookstonemeadows.org>
To: Melissa Cartwright <melissa.cartwright@uga.edu>
Date: November 3, 2023 9:11 PM
Subject: Proof of Fall issue – please verify phone

Hi Melissa,

Attached is the proof for the Fall 2023 Brookstone Bulletin. Before we send it to print, I need you to double-check the contact info box on page 2.

Right now it reads:

BROOKSTONE MEADOWS HOA
President: Melissa A. Cartwright
Email: brookstonemeadowshoa@gmail.com
Phone (call or text): 706-338-9041

Is 706-338-9041 still the best number for neighbors to reach you? I remember you mentioning you were thinking about moving everything over to a new cell, 470-281-6670, so you could keep work calls separate from HOA stuff and not have people ringing the UGA Office of Service-Learning line (706-542-1189) about potholes.

Also, the “Youth Spotlight” on Ellie and her classmates looks great, but I wanted to make sure you’re comfortable with the level of detail. You noted that Ellie has been seeing the school counselor twice a week since September 2023 because of anxiety after the bullying incident on the bus, and you included that photo of the three girls in front of the Barnett Shoals sign with their first and last names and homeroom teacher. Just checking one last time before we hit send.

Last, your draft comment about future neighborhood planning workshops mentions frustration that the Mayor and Commission tend to brush off resident input and asks about strategies for keeping certain kinds of student renters out of the subdivision. Do you want that in the “President’s Corner” or saved for a survey?

Please reply with any corrections tonight if possible.

Thanks,

Megan

metadata.subject: Proof of Fall issue – please verify phone

artifacts.content:
Title: Climate resilience workshop – prep call w/ Planning Dept
Date: April 29, 2025
Time: 10:30 AM – 11:15 AM
Location: UGA Office of Service-Learning (Zoom from office)
Attendees: Melissa; ACC Planning staff (TBD)

Description:

Goal: Prep for proposed neighborhood climate resilience workshop series.

Talking points to cover on the call:

1. Contact info logistics
- They’re asking for a “required information > telephone number” for each neighborhood point person.
- Clarify whether they prefer a personal cell or an organizational number.
- I’m leaning toward giving my cell, 470-281-6670, since that’s what neighbors already use, but I want to confirm I won’t end up on some public website. Alternative is my work line, 706-542-1189.

2. Example scenarios
- Share our April 2024 storms story: we had to pay nearly $3,800 to a Watkinsville contractor to re-grade the side yard and install a French drain because water was pooling against our foundation and seeping into the downstairs playroom where Jake keeps his things.
- Emphasize that other Brookstone Meadows homes are seeing similar issues.

3. Sensitive topics
- Ask how to handle situations where a family member is on the other side of a rezoning (e.g., Bob Cartwright investing in a Lexington Road multifamily project that might back up to us).
- Raise the idea of a segment on inclusive communication and privacy, especially around undocumented Spanish-speaking neighbors and my caregiving responsibilities for Mom.

4. Political tensions
- Carefully bring up that some residents feel the Mayor and Commission ignore neighborhood input and that there’s pressure from a few folks to find ways to keep certain types of student renters out.

Have ACC staff confirm what phone number they want on their internal contact sheet vs. anything public-facing.

metadata.title: Climate resilience workshop – prep call w/ Planning Dept
metadata.location: UGA Office of Service-Learning (Zoom from office)
